title: Capital, Volume I
license: Public domain in the United States; redistribution terms documented by Project Gutenberg.

sections.title: Commodities
paragraphs: The wealth of those societies in which the capitalist mode of production prevails, presents itself as an immense accumulation of commodities, its unit being a single commodity. Our investigation must therefore begin with the analysis of a commodity.
A commodity is, in the first place, an object outside us, a thing that by its properties satisfies human wants of some sort or another.

sections.title: Exchange
paragraphs: Commodities cannot go to market and make exchanges of their own account. We must, therefore, have recourse to their guardians, who are also their owners.
In order that these objects may enter into relation with each other as commodities, their guardians must place themselves in relation to one another as persons whose will resides in those objects.

sections.title: Money, or the Circulation of Commodities
paragraphs: Throughout this work, I assume, for the sake of simplicity, gold as the money commodity.
Money functions as a measure of value because it is the socially recognised incarnation of human labour.
